Flag parsing accepts positional target name OR --target/--target-path/--filter flags. Back-compat: --global = install_local + install_target claude-code; --global-pi = install_local + claude-code + pi + verify pi. New flags: --target NAME / --target-path PATH [--filter F] / --list-targets / --verify-firewall [NAME]. bash -n syntax clean.

Rationale: 'autonomously spawned agents first-class' (the Phase-5 charter) requires the parent-side spawn discipline to be embeddable in a runtime parent — not just dev-time. Spawn ledger = run_state/spawn.jsonl (append-only, parallel to run.jsonl; per-project). Multiple entries per spawn_id (latest-status-per-id is canonical view, matching proposals.jsonl pattern). Audit invariants designed in: contract immutable once written; budget mandatory; skill subset ⊆ runtime-safe by default; done-condition validated by [[validate]]; closed dep set on Layer A.

observable_expected: .agents/skills/spawn-contract/SKILL.md authored — Layer A runtime-safe; full contract schema; parent-side procedure; rules enforcing immutability + budget reality + closed authority
observable_actual: ~140-line SKILL.md (under the 200-line minimal-context cap). Six-step parent procedure: define task → constrain skill subset → set real budget → write contract → launch child (harness-agnostic) → wait/poll → reconcile (validate done_condition) → log closure. Five enforced rules: contract before launch, contract immutable, budget real, no self-extending authority, validate the done-condition, skill subset closed. Pairs with [[run-log]] [[validate]] [[gate-check]] [[fallback]] — all Layer A (closed dep set under the BOUNDARY S17.6 invocation-vs-documentation clarification). Cross-refs [[orchestrate]] as the dev-time-with-human complement. Harness picked up the skill within-session.
notes: first audit caught a 'Claude Code' mention violating the no-dev-harness-dep rule; generalized to harness-agnostic language during S21.6 validate

observable_expected: spawn-event schema documented; ledger path specified
observable_actual: Schema in SKILL.md: timestamp, spawn_id, parent_task_id, child_task_id, status (spawned|running|completed|escalated|aborted|budget_exceeded), contract block (task_statement, done_condition, skill_subset, authority_cap, budget{wall_time_seconds, iterations, cost_usd}, self_gating_rules, reporting_format, escalation_path), result block (child_summary, done_condition_check, verified_at, verified_by). Multiple lines per spawn_id; contract block immutable; result added in follow-up line. Ledger path: run_state/spawn.jsonl (append-only).
notes: S21.3 folded into S21.2 — the schema lives in the skill where it belongs

observable_expected: orchestrate SKILL.md gains a pointer at spawn-contract for the autonomous case
observable_actual: Added 6-line paragraph after 'This is a dev-time skill...' establishing the orchestrate/spawn-contract divide: orchestrate assumes human-watching multi-role flow; spawn-contract is the same decomposition with the human gap closed by an explicit, bounded, immutable contract. Doc-only reference (orchestrate does NOT invoke spawn-contract); orchestrate stays Layer C runtime-safe false.

observable_expected: run_state/spawn.jsonl seeded with a structural-mock spawn lifecycle (spawn → completed) demonstrating the contract pattern
observable_actual: SP-001 mock: parent task s21_5_worked_mock_spawn spawns child count_brain_edges to count typed edges in memory/brain/edges.jsonl. Contract: skill_subset=[validate,run-log]; authority=read-only on edges.jsonl + no network/shell/writes outside own log; budget=60s wall time; self-gating on malformed JSONL or unknown edge type. Parent reconciled: done_condition_check passed (count integer ≥0; by_type is dict; sum(by_type.values())==count; all keys in allowed set). 2 spawn.jsonl entries (spawned + completed); latest-per-spawn_id view shows status=completed.

observable_expected: pack model decided, per-skill assignments mapped
observable_actual: Four-pack model: core (7, always installed: Layer A + slip-ladder), meta (5, always installed: Layer C framework infra), research (8, opt-in: research lifecycle), brain (4, opt-in: observation/reflection). Pack is orthogonal to layer — Layer A is always pack=core; Layer B can be core (slip-ladder) or a vertical; Layer C is typically meta. Total 7+5+8+4=24 matches skill count.

observable_expected: every SKILL.md gains `pack: <value>` after the `runtime-safe:` line
observable_actual: One-shot Python script (/tmp/add_pack_frontmatter.py, deleted after run) inserted `pack:` to 24/24 SKILL.md files; 0 skipped, 0 errors. Idempotent design — re-runs would skip files already carrying pack. Verified per-skill: 7 core, 5 meta, 8 research, 4 brain. The harness re-picked up all skills with the new frontmatter field within-session.
